title: Ohinemutu

infobox.value: Te Ipu Wai Auraki General Ward

is a suburb in [Rotorua](/wiki/Rotorua "Rotorua"), [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand"). It includes a living Māori village and the original settlement of Rotorua. The organiser of a celebration for the area having been settled for 650 years said it has been continuously inhabited since 1365.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

["Ohinemutu marks nearly 650 years"](https://www.nzherald.co.nz/rotorua-daily-post/news/ohinemutu-marks-nearly-650-years/NKQBUDBPVMEHZKL43PM7BCERHI/). *[Rotorua Daily Post](/wiki/Rotorua_Daily_Post "Rotorua Daily Post")*. 15 November 2013.

["Statistical area 1 dataset for 2018 Census"](https://www.stats.govt.nz/information-releases/statistical-area-1-dataset-for-2018-census-updated-march-2020). Statistics New Zealand. March 2020. Kuirau (199800). [2018 Census place summary: Kuirau](https://www.stats.govt.nz/tools/2018-census-place-summaries/kuirau)

["Totals by topic for dwellings, (RC, TALB, UR, SA3, SA2, Ward, Health), 2013, 2018, and 2023 Censuses"](https://explore.data.stats.govt.nz/vis?fs%5b0%5d=2023%20Census%2C0%7CTotals%20by%20topic%23CAT_TOTALS_BY_TOPIC%23&pg=0&fc=Variable%20codes&bp=true&snb=9&df%5bds%5d=ds-nsiws-disseminate&df%5bid%5d=CEN23_TBT_005&df%5bag%5d=STATSNZ&df%5bvs%5d=1.0&dq=doTotal%2Bdo1.199800.2023&ly%5brw%5d=CEN23_TBT_DWD_003). Stats NZ – Tatauranga Aotearoa – Aotearoa Data Explorer. Retrieved 3 October 2024.

**Ohinemutu** is a suburb in [Rotorua](/wiki/Rotorua "Rotorua"), [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand"). It includes a living Māori village and the original settlement of Rotorua. The organiser of a celebration for the area having been settled for 650 years said it has been continuously inhabited since 1365.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ohinemutu&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Demographics")]

The statistical area of Kuirau, which corresponds to Ohinemutu, covers 1.21 km2 (0.47 sq mi)[[1]](#cite_note-Area-1) and had an estimated population of 1,100 as of June 2025,[[2]](#cite_note-NZ_population_data_2023_SA2-2) with a population density of 909 people per km2.

Historical population

| Year | Pop. | ±% p.a. |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 2006 | 927 | — |
| 2013 | 921 | −0.09% |
| 2018 | 1,065 | +2.95% |
| 2023 | 1,080 | +0.28% |
| Source: [[4]](#cite_note-Census_2023-4)[[5]](#cite_note-Census_2018-5) | | |

Kuirau had a population of 1,080 in the [2023 New Zealand census](/wiki/2023_New_Zealand_census "2023 New Zealand census"), an increase of 15 people (1.4%) since the [2018 census](/wiki/2018_New_Zealand_census "2018 New Zealand census"), and an increase of 159 people (17.3%) since the [2013 census](/wiki/2013_New_Zealand_census "2013 New Zealand census"). There were 549 males, 528 females, and 3 people of [other genders](/wiki/Non-binary_gender "Non-binary gender") in 429 dwellings.[[6]](#cite_note-6) 2.5% of people identified as [LGBTIQ+](/wiki/LGBTQ "LGBTQ"). The median age was 35.5 years (compared with 38.1 years nationally). There were 228 people (21.1%) aged under 15 years, 219 (20.3%) aged 15 to 29, 477 (44.2%) aged 30 to 64, and 156 (14.4%) aged 65 or older.[[4]](#cite_note-Census_2023-4)

People could identify as more than one ethnicity. The results were 35.0% [European](/wiki/European_New_Zealanders "European New Zealanders") ([Pākehā](/wiki/P%C4%81keh%C4%81 "Pākehā")); 67.8% [Māori](/wiki/M%C4%81ori_people "Māori people"); 9.4% [Pasifika](/wiki/Pasifika_New_Zealanders "Pasifika New Zealanders"); 14.4% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_New_Zealanders "Asian New Zealanders"); 0.3% Middle Eastern, Latin American and African New Zealanders (MELAA); and 0.6% other, which includes people giving their ethnicity as "New Zealander". English was spoken by 94.2%, Māori by 29.7%, and other languages by 11.4%. No language could be spoken by 2.5% (e.g. too young to talk). [New Zealand Sign Language](/wiki/New_Zealand_Sign_Language "New Zealand Sign Language") was known by 0.8%. The percentage of people born overseas was 18.3, compared with 28.8% nationally.[[4]](#cite_note-Census_2023-4)

Religious affiliations were 34.4% [Christian](/wiki/Christianity_in_New_Zealand "Christianity in New Zealand"), 3.3% [Hindu](/wiki/Hinduism_in_New_Zealand "Hinduism in New Zealand"), 0.3% [Islam](/wiki/Islam_in_New_Zealand "Islam in New Zealand"), 7.2% [Māori religious beliefs](/wiki/M%C4%81ori_religious_beliefs "Māori religious beliefs"), 0.8% [Buddhist](/wiki/Buddhism_in_New_Zealand "Buddhism in New Zealand"), 0.3% [New Age](/wiki/New_Age "New Age"), and 1.7% other religions. People who answered that they had [no religion](/wiki/Irreligion_in_New_Zealand "Irreligion in New Zealand") were 47.5%, and 5.6% of people did not answer the census question.[[4]](#cite_note-Census_2023-4)

Of those at least 15 years old, 168 (19.7%) people had a bachelor's or higher degree, 447 (52.5%) had a post-high school certificate or diploma, and 246 (28.9%) people exclusively held high school qualifications. The median income was $33,900, compared with $41,500 nationally. 45 people (5.3%) earned over $100,000 compared to 12.1% nationally. The employment status of those at least 15 was 396 (46.5%) full-time, 117 (13.7%) part-time, and 51 (6.0%) unemployed.[[4]](#cite_note-Census_2023-4)

## Marae

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ohinemutu&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Marae")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/54/Exterior_view_of_Te_Ao_Marama_meeting_house%2C_Ohinemutu%2C_used_by_the_Board_of_Maori_Arts_in_the_1930s._ATLIB_291203.png/250px-Exterior_view_of_Te_Ao_Marama_meeting_house%2C_Ohinemutu%2C_used_by_the_Board_of_Maori_Arts_in_the_1930s._ATLIB_291203.png)](/wiki/File:Exterior_view_of_Te_Ao_Marama_meeting_house,_Ohinemutu,_used_by_the_Board_of_Maori_Arts_in_the_1930s._ATLIB_291203.png)

Te Ao Marama meeting house in Ohinemutu in the 1930s

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/7c/The_Sunken_Pah%2C_Ohinemutu_SLNSW_FL10386959.jpg/250px-The_Sunken_Pah%2C_Ohinemutu_SLNSW_FL10386959.jpg)](/wiki/File:The_Sunken_Pah,_Ohinemutu_SLNSW_FL10386959.jpg)

The Sunken Pā, Ohinemutu SLNSW FL10386959

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/91/Ohinemutu.gif/250px-Ohinemutu.gif)](/wiki/File:Ohinemutu.gif)

1880 sketch of Ohinemutu

The Ohinemutu area has four [marae](/wiki/Marae "Marae"):

* Te Kuirau or Utuhina Marae and Te Roro o Te Rangi meeting house is a meeting place of the [Ngāti Whakaue](/wiki/Ng%C4%81ti_Whakaue "Ngāti Whakaue") [hapū](/wiki/Hap%C5%AB "Hapū") of [Ngāti te Roro o te Rangi](/wiki/Ng%C4%81ti_te_Roro_o_te_Rangi "Ngāti te Roro o te Rangi").[[7]](#cite_note-tkmentry-7)[[8]](#cite_note-maorimaps-8)
* [Te Papaiouru Marae](/wiki/Te_Papaiouru_Marae "Te Papaiouru Marae") and Tamatekapua meeting house is a meeting place of the [Ngāti Whakaue](/wiki/Ng%C4%81ti_Whakaue "Ngāti Whakaue") hapū of [Ngāti Hurunga Te Rangi](/wiki/Ng%C4%81ti_Hurunga_Te_Rangi "Ngāti Hurunga Te Rangi"), [Ngāti Pūkaki](/wiki/Ng%C4%81ti_P%C5%ABkaki "Ngāti Pūkaki"), [Ngāti Taeotu](/wiki/Ng%C4%81ti_Taeotu "Ngāti Taeotu"), [Ngāti Te Rangiwaho](/wiki/Ng%C4%81ti_Te_Rangiwaho "Ngāti Te Rangiwaho"), [Ngāti te Roro o te Rangi](/wiki/Ng%C4%81ti_te_Roro_o_te_Rangi "Ngāti te Roro o te Rangi") and [Ngāti Tūnohopū](/wiki/Ng%C4%81ti_T%C5%ABnohop%C5%AB "Ngāti Tūnohopū").[[7]](#cite_note-tkmentry-7)[[8]](#cite_note-maorimaps-8)
* Para te Hoata or Tūnohopū Marae and Tūnohopū meeting house is a meeting place of the [Ngāti Whakaue](/wiki/Ng%C4%81ti_Whakaue "Ngāti Whakaue") hapū of [Ngāti Tūnohopū](/wiki/Ng%C4%81ti_T%C5%ABnohop%C5%AB "Ngāti Tūnohopū") and [Ngāti Whakaue](/wiki/Ng%C4%81ti_Whakaue "Ngāti Whakaue").[[7]](#cite_note-tkmentry-7)[[8]](#cite_note-maorimaps-8)
* Waikite Marae and Tiki meeting house is a meeting place of the [Ngāti Whakaue](/wiki/Ng%C4%81ti_Whakaue "Ngāti Whakaue") hapū of [Ngāti Whakaue](/wiki/Ng%C4%81ti_Whakaue_(hap%C5%AB) "Ngāti Whakaue (hapū)").[[7]](#cite_note-tkmentry-7)[[8]](#cite_note-maorimaps-8)
